 {"id":"bd-17kf","title":"Implement Time-Travel Debugging tests","description":"# Implement Time-Travel Debugging tests

## Background
Part of Time-Travel Debugging (bd-2lwf). Tests ensure frame snapshots work correctly.

## Unit Tests
- snapshot_captures_full_frame_state: Buffer, cursor, style all captured
- delta_encoding_produces_smaller_output: Delta < full snapshot for similar frames
- decompression_matches_original: Decompress(compress(frame)) == frame
- navigation_moves_through_history: previous()/next() work correctly
- seek_to_index_works: seek(n) jumps to correct frame
- ring_buffer_evicts_oldest: Old frames evicted when capacity reached

## Property Tests
- delta_roundtrip_is_lossless: Any frame survives delta encode/decode cycle
- frame_ordering_is_preserved: Frames come out in insertion order

## Integration Tests
- full_session_recording_and_replay: Record session, scrub through history
- export_to_file_and_reimport: Save history to disk, load it back

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples
- [ ] Memory usage bounded by ring buffer size
- [ ] Frame navigation is smooth (< 1ms per seek)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:24.133973381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:16:08.661931554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:16:08.661911236Z","close_reason":"TimeTravel tests already implemented in crates/ftui-harness/src/time_travel.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17kf","depends_on_id":"bd-2lwf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-17nn","title":"Widget Introspection Debug Overlay","description":"# Widget Introspection Debug Overlay

## Background & Motivation

When developing complex widget layouts, developers need to see:
- Widget boundaries
- Widget names and types
- Render times per widget
- Hit test areas for mouse events

**The solution:**
- Debug overlay toggleable at runtime (F12 or config)
- Zero overhead when disabled
- Rich visual feedback during development

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.7 (Widgets Ring) for widget tree access
- Complements bd-9xbn (Layout Constraint Debugger)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct DebugOverlay {
    enabled: bool,
    show_boundaries: bool,
    show_names: bool,
    show_render_times: bool,
    show_hit_areas: bool,
}

impl DebugOverlay {
    pub fn wrap<W: Widget>(widget: W, name: &str) -> DebugWrapped<W> { ... }
    
    pub fn render_overlay(&self, frame: &Frame, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Draw colored rectangles around each widget
        // Show widget names as labels
        // Show render time in corner
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Overlay toggle (INFO level)
tracing::info!(enabled = enabled, "Debug overlay toggled");

// Widget render time captured (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(
    widget = name,
    render_time_us = render_time.as_micros(),
    area = ?area,
    "Widget render complete"
);
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Enable overlay and take screenshot
./ftui-harness --debug-overlay --screenshot overlay.png

# Verify overlay labels visible
./scripts/verify-overlay.sh overlay.png --expect-widgets "Panel,Button,Input"

# Performance test with overlay
./ftui-harness --debug-overlay --benchmark --assert-overhead-percent 5
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Widget boundaries visible with distinct colors
- [ ] Widget names displayed as labels
- [ ] Render times shown in microseconds
- [ ] Toggle without code changes (F12 or env var)
- [ ] Zero overhead when disabled (compile-time check)
- [ ] Hit areas highlighted on hover
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"Implemented debug overlay widget + stateful wrappers with env-var default enable, F12 toggle helper, hover hit-area highlight, and tracing logs for toggle/render time. Added debug-overlay feature gate + exports. Added unit tests in module. Workspace checks currently failing due to pre-existing issues in ftui-core logging macros, ftui-style env var safety, ftui-pty clippy, and fmt diff in ftui-render/buffer.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:11:10.574626039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:53.490978156Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:53.490899988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17nn","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-17rz1","title":"Render: layout integration + caching/invalidation","description":"Goal
- Integrate layout computation with caching and explicit invalidation triggers.

Deliverables
- Layout modes (Auto/Dense/Spacious) respected.
- Cache invalidated on relevant state changes only.

Acceptance
- Layout recompute happens only when needed and updates metrics.

 {"id":"bd-17w24","title":"Mermaid Styling Support (classDef/style/linkStyle)","description":"Background
- Mermaid diagrams use styling directives to convey semantics.
- Terminal constraints mean we map styles to fg/bg/border/accent + glyph choices.

Scope
- Parse classDef/class/style/linkStyle directives from AST/Init.
- Resolve style precedence (node-specific > class > global defaults).
- Apply init themeVariables where supported (mapped to theme tokens).
- Map styles to theme tokens and rendering primitives (fg/bg, border, line style, arrowhead style).
- Clamp to accessible contrast where possible (prevent low-contrast combos).
- Provide graceful fallback for unsupported styles with warnings (compat matrix).

Considerations
- Deterministic mapping; same styles → same tokens.
- Avoid invalid color combos; clamp to theme palette.
- Log style resolution and fallback decisions in evidence logs.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for precedence resolution + style mapping.
- Snapshot tests for styled nodes/edges across Unicode/ASCII modes.

Success Criteria
- Styles applied where supported; unsupported styles degrade predictably.","notes":"Implemented linkStyle index correctness by preserving edge order (edge_drafts sorted by insertion_idx), added test resolve_styles_linkstyle_respects_edge_order, updated docs/spec/mermaid-config.md Style Resolution section. Quality gates: cargo fmt/check/clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:38:48.238250324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:29:06.691586332Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:44:56.395100453Z","close_reason":"Completed: CSS property parser (MermaidColor, MermaidStyleProperties), style resolution engine with class<node precedence, IrEdge style_ref field, linkStyle support (default + indexed), WCAG 2.0 contrast clamping, 14 unit tests. All 53 mermaid tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17w24","depends_on_id":"bd-1ri6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:41:53.320483233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17w24","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:10:02.276330040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17w24","depends_on_id":"bd-37xrr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:05:32.190183537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17w24","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:41:49.260511768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1csc.4","title":"Keyboard Drag Support","description":"Enable drag operations via keyboard for accessibility.

## Requirements
- Space/Enter to pick up item
- Arrow keys to navigate targets
- Space/Enter to drop
- Escape to cancel

## Implementation
- KeyboardDragMode state
- Visual target highlight during kb drag
- Announce drag state for screen readers

## Tests
- Full keyboard drag flow
- Cancel behavior
- Accessibility announcements

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Keyboard Drag Support
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","notes":"Implementation complete in keyboard_drag.rs. Module implements keyboard-driven drag-and-drop support with:
- KeyboardDragMode enum (Inactive/Holding/Navigating)
- KeyboardDragManager with target navigation
- Direction-based navigation (arrows)
- Space/Enter activation, Escape cancellation
- Visual target highlighting with pulse animation
- Accessibility announcements queue
- Comprehensive test suite (25+ tests)

BLOCKED: Tests cannot run due to compilation errors in modal/dialog.rs (another agent's in-progress work on bd-39vx.1 Modal Container Widget). Module provides: KeyboardDragMode enum, KeyboardDragManager with target navigation, Direction-based navigation (arrows), Space/Enter activation, Escape cancellation, visual target highlighting with pulse animation, accessibility announcements queue.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:30:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1csc.5","title":"Drag-and-Drop Demo","description":"Demo showcasing drag-and-drop features.

## Requirements
- Sortable list demo
- Cross-container drag
- Keyboard drag demo
- Various payload types

## Implementation
- DragDropDemo screen in showcase
- Multiple drag scenarios
- Accessibility instructions panel

## Success Criteria
- Both mouse and keyboard drag work
- Clear visual feedback
- No dropped events

- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

Demo includes three modes: (1) Sortable List - reorder items with u/d keys, (2) Cross-Container - transfer items between lists with Enter, (3) Keyboard Drag - full keyboard-accessible drag using Space/Enter to pick up, arrows to navigate, Enter to drop. 9 unit tests cover initial state, mode navigation, selection, item movement, transfer, and rendering. Integrated into app.rs with ScreenId::DragDrop.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:38:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1csc.6","title":"Drag-and-Drop E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of drag-and-drop functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Mouse Drag (dnd_mouse_e2e.rs)
- Drag initiation threshold
- Drag preview follows cursor
- Drop zone highlighting
- Drop acceptance/rejection

### 2. Keyboard Drag (dnd_keyboard_e2e.rs)
- Pick up with Space/Enter
- Navigate targets with arrows
- Drop with Space/Enter
- Cancel with Escape

### 3. Cross-Container (dnd_cross_e2e.rs)
- Drag between lists
- Type compatibility checking
- Drop position (before/after/inside)
- Drag into nested containers

### 4. Sortable Lists (dnd_sortable_e2e.rs)
- Reorder within list
- Visual placeholder
- Auto-scroll during drag
- Drag handle mode

### 5. Edge Cases (dnd_edge_e2e.rs)
- Drag cancel mid-flight
- Drop on disabled target
- Nested drop targets
- Long distance drag scroll

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Drag lifecycle (start, move, end, cancel)
- DEBUG: Hit testing calculations
- TRACE: Position updates per frame

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/drag_drop/

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Drag-and-Drop E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:53.651269891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:41:39.118418235Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:41:39.118399470Z","close_reason":"Completed: existing drag_drop E2E suite verified (all cases pass)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1csc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1csc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1csc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1csc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1dhk1","title":"Fix duplicate MouseCapture / set_mouse_capture in ftui-runtime","description":"Current ftui-runtime does not compile: duplicate enum variants and duplicate methods around mouse capture. Downstream builds (e.g. mcp_agent_mail_rust cargo check --all-targets) fail with E0428 (MouseCapture defined multiple times), E0592 (set_mouse_capture defined multiple times), plus unreachable_patterns + non-exhaustive match errors.

Fix plan:
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/simulator.rs: remove the duplicated CmdRecord::MouseCapture(bool) variant; update derives/matches to be exhaustive.
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs: remove duplicate Program::set_mouse_capture; remove duplicate Cmd::SetMouseCapture match arms (causing unreachable patterns).

Acceptance:
- cargo check -p ftui-runtime --all-targets passes.
- cargo clippy -p ftui-runtime --all-targets -- -D warnings passes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:38:10.482252509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:42:59.733383820Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:42:59.733365065Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compile","ftui-runtime"],"comments":[{"id":822,"issue_id":"bd-1dhk1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Investigated — HEAD (f4c16e42) compiles cleanly with 'cargo check -p ftui-runtime --all-targets'. The duplicate MouseCapture/SetMouseCapture variants were injected by PostToolUse hooks, not in committed code. This is a hooks artifact, not a real bug in the codebase. Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:42:59Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2hxl1","title":"Edge Routing Engine (Orthogonal + Obstacle-Aware)","description":"Background\n- Terminal diagrams need orthogonal routing with minimal crossings.\n- Edges must avoid node boxes and reserved label zones.\n\nScope\n- Routing grid with obstacles for nodes + labels.\n- A* search with penalties for bends, crossings, and length.\n- Deterministic port selection with stable tie-breaks.\n- Route simplification and grid alignment.\n- Handle self-loops, multi-edges, and parallel edges with offset lanes.\n- Optional edge bundling for parallel segments to reduce visual clutter.\n\nConsiderations\n- Deterministic results even under symmetric graphs.\n- Provide routing diagnostics (cost, bends, crossings) for evidence logs.\n\nTests / Validation\n- Unit tests for routing on canonical layouts.\n- Regression tests for obstacle and parallel-edge cases.\n\nSuccess Criteria\n- Routes avoid nodes/labels and remain within bounds.\n- Bend count and crossings minimized under constraints.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Opus","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:39:03.744427318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:06:34.728411908Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:06:34.728341435Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:41:59.217046520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","depends_on_id":"bd-2oovu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:01.246976429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":691,"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Edge Routing Engine implemented and tested. Key additions/fixes:

1. **A* Search with Bend Penalties** (find_path_astar): Obstacle-aware routing through RouteGrid with configurable weights for step cost, bend penalties, and crossing penalties. Direction-tracked state space (5 layers: Up/Down/Left/Right/Start).
2. **Self-Loop Routing** (self_loop_route): Direction-aware self-loop path generation for TB/TD/BT/LR/RL directions.
3. **Parallel Edge Offsets** (parallel_edge_offset): Deterministic perpendicular offsets for parallel edges between same node pairs.
4. **Full Routing Pipeline** (route_all_edges): Processes all diagram edges with self-loop detection, parallel edge grouping, route budget enforcement, crossing tracking via occupied_routes bitmap.
5. **Routing Diagnostics** (RouteDiagnostics, RoutingReport): Per-edge cost, bends, cells explored, fallback status; aggregated totals.
6. **Objective Scoring** (evaluate_layout): Composite quality metric with crossings, bends, position variance, edge length, alignment.
7. **Constraint-Based Compaction** (compact_positions): Longest-path compaction shifting nodes toward neighbor centers.
8. **Default Implementations**: Added Default for MermaidInitParse, MermaidGuardReport, MermaidComplexity, MermaidDegradationPlan, MermaidFidelity.

Fixed compilation errors in test code (reference vs value for LayoutPoint in find_path_astar calls).

41 tests pass. All quality gates clean (fmt, check, clippy).","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:06:13Z"},{"id":692,"issue_id":"bd-2hxl1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BlueOtter: Implemented edge routing engine in mermaid_layout.rs.  {"id":"bd-2ixu","title":"Final verification: full build, tests, and integration check","description":"## Scope

Final verification that the Widget API migration is complete and correct.

## Verification Steps

### 1. Full Workspace Build

```bash
cargo check --workspace
cargo build --workspace
```

All crates must compile without errors.

### 2. Test Suite

```bash
cargo test --workspace
```

All tests must pass.

### 3. Clippy

```bash
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
```

No new warnings introduced.

### 4. Cross-Crate Integration

Verify crates that depend on ftui-widgets still work:
- ftui-extras (canvas, charts, forms features)
- ftui-harness (dev-dependency)
- ftui (main crate)

### 5. Documentation

```bash
cargo doc --workspace --no-deps
```

Documentation builds without errors.

### 6. Feature Combinations

Test key feature combinations:

```bash
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features canvas
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features forms
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features charts
```

## Success Criteria

- ✓ `cargo check --workspace` passes
- ✓ `cargo test --workspace` passes
- ✓ `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- ✓ No regressions in functionality
- ✓ All dependent crates compile

## If Issues Found

Create new beads for any issues discovered during verification.
This bead is only marked complete when ALL checks pass.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:23:37.279584061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:35:58.826218081Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:35:35.076295382Z","close_reason":"All verification checks pass. Widget API migration complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-10y0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-1abx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-1rqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-2pip","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","depends_on_id":"bd-z8q6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":367,"issue_id":"bd-2ixu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Verification Execution Plan

### Unit Test Requirements (with detailed logging)

1. **Widget Signature Tests** - Verify all Widget impls use Frame:
   ```bash
   # Run with detailed logging
   RUST_LOG=debug cargo test -p ftui-widgets -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee widget_tests.log
   ```

2. **Frame Feature Tests** - Test hit_grid, cursor, degradation:
   ```bash
   RUST_LOG=debug cargo test -p ftui-render frame -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee frame_tests.log
   ```

3. **Integration Tests** - Cross-crate compilation:
   ```bash
   cargo check --workspace 2>&1 | tee workspace_check.log
   ```

### E2E Test Script
Create `scripts/widget_migration_e2e.sh`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Widget Migration E2E Test ==="
echo "[1/6] Workspace build..."
cargo build --workspace 2>&1 | tee build.log
echo "[2/6] Unit tests..."
cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | tee test.log
echo "[3/6] Clippy..."
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee clippy.log
echo "[4/6] Feature combinations..."
cargo check -p ftui-extras --features canvas,forms,charts 2>&1 | tee features.log
echo "[5/6] Documentation build..."
cargo doc --workspace --no-deps 2>&1 | tee doc.log
echo "[6/6] Widget grep verification..."
# Verify no old Buffer signatures remain
if rg 'fn render.*buf: &mut Buffer' crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs; then
  echo "ERROR: Found old Buffer signature!"
  exit 1
fi
echo "All verifications passed!"
```

### Success Criteria with Logging
- [ ] build.log shows no errors
- [ ] test.log shows all tests pass
- [ ] clippy.log shows no warnings
- [ ] features.log shows all feature combos compile
- [ ] doc.log shows docs build
- [ ] No old Buffer signatures found

 {"id":"bd-2j6fz","title":"perf(canvas): add #[inline] to drawing primitives (line, rect, circle, polygon)","description":"The canvas drawing primitives line(), line_colored(), rect(), rect_filled(), polygon_filled(), circle(), and plot_circle_octants() are missing #[inline] hints. These are called from widget rendering hot paths (mermaid, charts, etc.) and delegate to point()/point_colored() in tight loops. Adding #[inline] enables the compiler to inline the small wrappers and hoist bounds checks. Follows the same pattern as bd-1r1qb which added #[inline] to point/point_colored.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildCave","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:58:14.951580896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:01:05.845094853Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:01:05.845025633Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2j71","title":"Text Effects: Vertical and Diagonal Gradients","description":"## Background
Current gradients only support horizontal direction. Vertical, diagonal, and radial gradients complete the gradient system and enable effects like sunset (orange bottom, purple top).

## Implementation

Add new TextEffect variants:
```rust
TextEffect::VerticalGradient {
    gradient: ColorGradient,
    // For multi-line: t = row / total_rows
    // For single-line: uses text height in cells (e.g., ASCII art)
}

TextEffect::DiagonalGradient {
    gradient: ColorGradient,
    angle: f64,  // degrees: 0=right, 90=down, 180=left, 270=up
}

TextEffect::RadialGradient {
    gradient: ColorGradient,
    center: (f64, f64),  // normalized 0-1, (0.5, 0.5) = center
    aspect: f64,         // 1.0 = circular, 2.0 = horizontally stretched
}
```

### Coordinate Mapping:

| Type | t Calculation |
|------|---------------|
| Horizontal | char_idx / total_chars |
| Vertical (multi-line) | row_idx / total_rows |
| Vertical (single-line) | 0.5 (center color) OR uses AsciiArt height |
| Diagonal | (x*cos(θ) + y*sin(θ)) normalized to 0-1 |
| Radial | distance_from_center / max_distance |

### Animation Support:
All gradient variants can be animated by adding time offset:
```rust
// In StyledText, if gradient effect + time > 0:
let animated_t = (t + self.time * speed).rem_euclid(1.0);
```

### Single-Line Vertical Gradient Behavior:
For single-row text, vertical gradient returns gradient.sample(0.5) for all chars (middle of gradient). To get full vertical gradient effect, use multi-line text or AsciiArtText.

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_vertical_multiline_top_is_start: Row 0 gets sample(0)
- test_vertical_multiline_bottom_is_end: Last row gets sample(1)
- test_vertical_singleline_is_middle: All chars get sample(0.5)
- test_diagonal_0_is_horizontal: Matches HorizontalGradient
- test_diagonal_90_is_vertical: Top-to-bottom sweep
- test_diagonal_angle_wraps: 450° same as 90°
- test_radial_center_is_start: Center gets sample(0)
- test_radial_corner_is_end: Corners get sample(1)
- test_radial_aspect_stretches: aspect=2 makes ellipse
- test_gradient_animated: Time offset moves gradient

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot vertical on 5-line AsciiArt text
- Snapshot diagonal at 45°
- Snapshot radial with aspect=1 vs aspect=2

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Vertical and Diagonal Gradients
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-2k018","title":"Modular Table Theme System (All Tables)","description":"Goal:
- Unify table styling across all tables (widget tables + markdown tables) with a modular, animatable theming system.

Background / Problem:
- Styling is currently split: MarkdownTheme has table fields while widget tables have their own styling path.
- Users want consistent visuals, easy customization, tasteful animation, and clear preset selection guidance.

Design principles:
- Deterministic by default: animation uses explicit phase/time input (no implicit clocks).
- Lightweight: style resolution is O(1) per row/column; avoid hot-path allocations.
- No compatibility shims: adopt the new system directly (project preference).
- Presets should be readable at high density and a11y-friendly.

Deliverables:
- Shared TableTheme struct (border/header/row/alt/selected/hover + spacing/dividers).
- Effect hooks with row/column/section targeting and phase-driven transforms.
- Presets (aurora/graphite/neon/slate/solar/orchard/paper/midnight/terminal_classic) with strong defaults + easy overrides.
- Integrations in widget tables and markdown tables.
- Demo showcase with subtle animated highlight row.
- Preset preview gallery screen to compare presets side-by-side.
- Comprehensive unit tests, snapshots, and E2E scripts with structured logging.
- Public API ergonomics (builder helpers + re-exports) so customization is trivial.

Acceptance:
- All table render paths can be themed via one struct + optional animation phase.
- Presets look great out of the box; customization is ergonomic.
- Animation is tasteful, unobtrusive, deterministic.
- Tests + E2E logs prove correctness and prevent regressions.
","notes":"Fixed test failures in table_theme.rs: effect_resolver_all_rows_excludes_header and effect_resolver_all_cells_includes_header_rows - the pulse_effect helper takes (fg, bg) but tests had parameters swapped. Also added TableThemeSpec to public exports in ftui-style/src/lib.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:11.758412732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:22:10.031334682Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:22:10.031312250Z","close_reason":"All TableTheme child beads are closed; system integrated + deterministic tests/E2E logs validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
